metadata.name: Jiu Jitsu Bench Glossary
metadata.description: Position-first glossary for a Jiu Jitsu reasoning benchmark. Japanese/Judo terms are included only when a standard term exists.
metadata.source_of_truth: JSON; SQLite is generated from this file.
metadata.ruleset_scope: Adult black-belt sport Jiu Jitsu, with gi and no-gi categories tracked separately where relevant.

terms.name: Mount
terms.definition: Top player sits on the opponent's torso with knees or feet controlling both sides of the body.

terms.name: Low mount
terms.definition: Mount variation with the top player's hips lower on the opponent, commonly used to stabilize before climbing higher.

terms.name: High mount
terms.definition: Mount variation with the top player's knees climbed high toward the opponent's armpits to isolate the arms.

terms.name: Technical mount
terms.definition: Side-facing mount used when the bottom player turns, with one knee high near the shoulder and the other leg posted or hooking near the hip.

terms.name: S-mount
terms.definition: High mount variation where the top player's legs form an S-like shape to isolate an arm for armbars and triangles.

terms.name: Reverse mount
terms.definition: Mount variation where the top player faces the opponent's legs rather than the head.

terms.name: Side control
terms.definition: Top pin from the side of the opponent's torso, usually chest-to-chest or hip-to-hip, with the guard passed.

terms.name: North-south
terms.definition: Top pin with the players aligned head-to-head in opposite directions after a guard pass or side-control transition.

terms.name: Scarf hold
terms.definition: Side pin where the top player controls the head and near arm while facing toward the opponent's legs.

terms.name: Knee on belly
terms.definition: Top control with one knee applying pressure across the opponent's torso and the other foot posted for mobility.

terms.name: Knee on neck or shoulder
terms.definition: Pressure-position variation of knee ride where the knee is placed higher near the shoulder line or neck.

terms.name: Back control
terms.definition: Control from behind the opponent, usually with hooks, a body triangle, or another lower-body control preventing the opponent from turning in.

terms.name: Flattened back control
terms.definition: Back control where the opponent is forced belly-down and extended, often increasing choke and arm-trap pressure.

terms.name: Body triangle back control
terms.definition: Back control with the attacker's legs locked around the opponent's torso as a triangle.

terms.name: Turtle
terms.definition: Defensive grounded position where the bottom player is on knees and elbows or hands, facing the mat.

terms.name: Front headlock from turtle
terms.definition: Control over the head and often an arm while the opponent is turtled or partially turtled.

terms.name: Crucifix
terms.definition: Control position where one of the opponent's arms is trapped with the legs while the upper body is controlled from the side or back.

terms.name: T-Kimura
terms.definition: Kimura-based control variation associated with crucifix and back-exposure attacks.

terms.name: Truck
terms.definition: 10th Planet leg-ride control used for back takes, twister attacks, calf slicers, and related transitions.

terms.name: Baby bolo
terms.definition: Smaller berimbolo-style back-exposure pathway, commonly arising from De la Riva, leg-drag, or seated guard exchanges.

terms.name: Closed guard
terms.definition: Bottom guard with the legs locked around the opponent's waist or torso.

terms.name: High guard
terms.definition: Closed guard variation where the bottom player's legs climb high toward the opponent's shoulders to isolate posture and arms.

terms.name: Williams guard / overhook closed guard
terms.definition: Closed guard variation built around an overhook and shoulder control, commonly used to attack triangles, omoplatas, and armbars.

terms.name: Rubber guard
terms.definition: 10th Planet closed-guard system using leg and posture control to create choking, omoplata, and triangle threats.

terms.name: Half guard
terms.definition: Guard where the bottom player controls one of the top player's legs between their own legs.

terms.name: Knee shield half guard
terms.definition: Half guard variation using the top knee or shin as a frame across the opponent's torso, shoulder, or hip.

terms.name: Z-guard
terms.definition: Knee-shield half guard variation with the shin framing across the opponent, creating a Z-like leg structure.

terms.name: Deep half guard
terms.definition: Half guard where the bottom player is deep underneath the opponent's hips, often controlling a leg from below.

terms.name: Lockdown
terms.definition: 10th Planet half-guard leg configuration that stretches and immobilizes the trapped leg.

terms.name: Electric chair
terms.definition: 10th Planet lockdown pathway that can function as a sweep, control, or groin-stretch submission depending on context.

terms.name: Dogfight
terms.definition: Kneeling half-guard scramble position, usually with an underhook, where both players fight for the sweep, pass, or back exposure.

terms.name: Reverse half guard
terms.definition: Half guard orientation where the top player faces toward the opponent's legs rather than head.

terms.name: Quarter guard
terms.definition: Late-stage guard retention position where the bottom player traps part of one of the top player's legs, often near the ankle or lower shin.

terms.name: Three-quarter mount
terms.definition: Near-mount passing position where the top player has mostly cleared guard but one lower leg remains trapped.

terms.name: Open guard
terms.definition: Guard family where the bottom player's legs are not locked around the opponent, allowing mobile frames, hooks, grips, and entanglements.

terms.name: Seated guard
terms.definition: Open guard orientation where the bottom player is upright or semi-upright, often looking to wrestle up, enter legs, or off-balance.

terms.name: Supine open guard
terms.definition: Open guard orientation where the bottom player is on their back using legs, frames, and grips to manage distance.

terms.name: Butterfly guard
terms.definition: Seated open guard using inside hooks to lift, off-balance, sweep, or enter leg and upper-body attacks.

terms.name: Shin-to-shin guard
terms.definition: Open guard connection where the bottom player's shin is placed against the opponent's shin to enter single-leg X, wrestle up, or off-balance.

terms.name: X guard
terms.definition: Open guard where the bottom player's legs form an X-like structure around one of the standing opponent's legs.

terms.name: Reverse X guard
terms.definition: X guard variation with reversed leg orientation, commonly used for sweeps and leg-lock entries.

terms.name: De la Riva guard
terms.definition: Open guard using an outside leg hook around the opponent's lead leg, commonly paired with sleeve, collar, ankle, or wrist control.

terms.name: Reverse De la Riva guard
terms.definition: Open guard using an inside hook around the opponent's lead leg, often against knee-cut and headquarters passing.

terms.name: Spider guard
terms.definition: Gi open guard using sleeve grips and feet on the opponent's arms or biceps to control posture and distance.

terms.name: Lasso guard
terms.definition: Gi open guard where the bottom player's leg wraps around an opponent's arm controlled by a sleeve grip.

terms.name: Collar-sleeve guard
terms.definition: Gi open guard using a collar grip and sleeve grip to manage distance, break posture, and attack triangles, armbars, and omoplatas.

terms.name: Worm guard
terms.definition: Modern lapel guard where the bottom player feeds and controls a lapel around the opponent's leg to restrict movement and create sweeps.

terms.name: Lapel guard
terms.definition: Gi guard family built around feeding and controlling the opponent's lapel to create frames, binds, and off-balances.

terms.name: K-guard
terms.definition: Open guard leg-entry position where the bottom player creates an angled knee frame and hip access to enter backside 50/50, matrix, or other leg entanglements.

terms.name: Matrix guard
terms.definition: Inversion-based back-exposure pathway often linked to K-guard, De la Riva, and backside attacks.

terms.name: Tornado guard
terms.definition: Inverted guard variation used to create sweeps, back exposure, and omoplata-style angles.

terms.name: Donkey guard
terms.definition: Fringe inverted guard posture where the guard player turns away while inviting engagement.

terms.name: Ashi garami
terms.definition: Leg entanglement family where the attacker controls one of the opponent's legs with their own legs and hips.

terms.name: Outside ashi
terms.definition: Ashi garami variation with the attacker's legs positioned to the outside of the opponent's trapped leg.

terms.name: Cross ashi
terms.definition: Cross-body leg entanglement where the opponent's leg is controlled across the attacker's centerline.

terms.name: Inside sankaku
terms.definition: Leg entanglement also called saddle, honey hole, or 411, with strong control over the opponent's hip and knee line.
Mutual leg entanglement where both players have similar access to each other's legs.
Leg entanglement related to 50/50 where one player has an asymmetric hip and knee-line advantage.
Leg entanglement variation that improves attacking angle and control compared with neutral 50/50.

terms.name: Guard passing
terms.definition: Concept family for top-player positions and movements used to clear the opponent's guard.

terms.name: Headquarters
terms.definition: Top passing position usually with one knee or shin between the guard player's legs, used to threaten knee cuts, leg weaves, and smash passes.

terms.name: Combat base
terms.definition: Postured base with one knee up and one knee down, often used inside closed or open guard to manage distance and begin passing.

terms.name: Knee cut position
terms.definition: Passing position where the top player drives a knee across the guard player's thigh line to split and clear the guard.

terms.name: Half guard top
terms.definition: Top position inside the opponent's half guard, balancing pass pressure with submission and backstep threats.

terms.name: Smash half guard
terms.definition: Top half guard variation using chest, shoulder, and hip pressure to flatten the bottom player before passing.

terms.name: Leg drag
terms.definition: Passing position where the top player drags the opponent's leg across the centerline to expose the hip and back.

terms.name: Double-under stack
terms.definition: Passing position with both arms under the guard player's legs, stacking their hips toward their head.

terms.name: Over-under pass position
terms.definition: Pressure passing position with one arm over and one arm under the guard player's legs.

terms.name: Toreando / bullfighter passing position
terms.definition: Open-guard passing position using leg control and lateral movement to clear the legs.

terms.name: Body lock passing position
terms.definition: No-gi passing position where the top player locks around the guard player's torso to restrict hip movement.

terms.name: Folding pass position
terms.definition: Passing position that folds the guard player's knees and hips toward their torso to pin and clear the legs.

terms.name: Stack pass position
terms.definition: Passing position using pressure to stack the guard player's hips over their shoulders.

terms.name: Underhook
terms.definition: Arm position where a player's arm passes under the opponent's arm to control the shoulder line, torso, or angle.

terms.name: Overhook
terms.definition: Arm position where a player's arm wraps over the opponent's arm to limit posture, grip, or shoulder movement.

terms.name: Crossface
terms.definition: Shoulder or arm pressure across the opponent's face or jawline to turn the head and limit spinal alignment.

terms.name: Inside position
terms.definition: Positional advantage where a player's limbs occupy the inside channels, improving frames, pummeling, and control.

terms.name: Frame
terms.definition: Structural use of the limbs to create distance, carry weight, or block movement.

terms.name: Hook
terms.definition: Use of the foot or leg to attach to and manipulate the opponent's leg, hip, or torso.

terms.name: Seatbelt
terms.definition: Back-control upper-body grip with one arm over the shoulder and one arm under the armpit.

terms.name: Body triangle
terms.definition: Leg triangle around the opponent's torso, commonly used from back control to restrict breathing and hip rotation.

terms.name: Gift wrap
terms.definition: Control where one of the opponent's arms is forced across their centerline and held near the far side of the head or shoulder.

terms.name: Kimura trap
terms.definition: Figure-four shoulder control used to attack the Kimura, back takes, sweeps, and positional transitions.

terms.name: Kimura side control
terms.definition: Side-control variation built around a Kimura grip and shoulder isolation.

terms.name: Omoplata control
terms.definition: Control position where the attacker's leg traps the opponent's shoulder for an omoplata finish, sweep, or transition.

terms.name: Arm triangle control
terms.definition: Head-and-arm control where the opponent's shoulder is driven into one side of their neck.

terms.name: Triangle control
terms.definition: Leg configuration around the opponent's neck and one arm used to control posture and finish triangle chokes or transition.

terms.name: Armbar control
terms.definition: Finishing position for a straight arm lock with the elbow controlled against the attacker's hips.

terms.name: Mounted triangle control
terms.definition: Triangle configuration applied from mount or a mount transition.

terms.name: Back triangle control
terms.definition: Triangle configuration from the back that traps the opponent's torso, shoulder, or neck line.

terms.name: North-south choke control
terms.definition: Chest and shoulder control from north-south used to compress the opponent's neck without a gi grip.

terms.name: Guillotine / front headlock control
terms.definition: Head-and-arm control family used to attack guillotines, front headlock chokes, and transitions to the back.

terms.name: Darce / anaconda control
terms.definition: Arm-in front headlock control family that threads around the neck and trapped arm.

terms.name: Collar grip
terms.definition: Grip on the opponent's gi collar used to control posture, distance, and choking lines.

terms.name: Sleeve grip
terms.definition: Grip on the opponent's gi sleeve used to control an arm, prevent posting, or create guard attacks.

terms.name: Lapel grip
terms.definition: Grip on the opponent's lapel, including fed-lapel controls used in modern lapel guards.

terms.name: Belt grip
terms.definition: Grip on the opponent's belt used for control, retention, passing, or back exposure.

terms.name: Pant grip
terms.definition: Grip on the opponent's gi pants used to control leg position and passing or guard-retention exchanges.

terms.name: Armbar
terms.definition: Straight arm lock attacking elbow extension.

terms.name: Kimura
terms.definition: Figure-four shoulder lock using internal rotation of the shoulder.

terms.name: Americana
terms.definition: Figure-four shoulder lock using external rotation of the shoulder.

terms.name: Omoplata
terms.definition: Shoulder lock using the legs to trap and rotate the opponent's shoulder.

terms.name: Triangle choke
terms.definition: Leg choke trapping the opponent's neck and one arm.

terms.name: Rear naked choke
terms.definition: Choke from back control applied without using the gi.

terms.name: Arm triangle choke
terms.definition: Head-and-arm choke using the opponent's shoulder to compress one side of the neck.

terms.name: North-south choke
terms.definition: Choke from north-south using shoulder, chest, and head positioning to compress the neck.

terms.name: Guillotine choke
terms.definition: Front headlock choke attacking the neck from in front of the opponent.

terms.name: Darce choke
terms.definition: Arm-in choke threaded from the front headlock or side-control family.

terms.name: Anaconda choke
terms.definition: Arm-in front headlock choke that wraps around the neck and trapped arm.

terms.name: Cross-collar choke
terms.definition: Gi choke using crossed collar grips to compress the neck.

terms.name: Bow-and-arrow choke
terms.definition: Gi choke from back control using collar and leg or body positioning to create bow-and-arrow tension.

terms.name: Baseball bat choke
terms.definition: Gi choke using two collar grips in a baseball-bat-like hand configuration.

terms.name: Ezekiel choke
terms.definition: Choke commonly using the attacker's sleeve and forearm pressure, often from mount or inside guard.

terms.name: Loop choke
terms.definition: Gi collar choke using a looping arm action around the opponent's neck.

terms.name: Gogoplata
terms.definition: Shin-based choke usually attacked from high guard or rubber guard.

terms.name: Straight ankle lock
terms.definition: Foot and ankle lock attacking extension and pressure through the ankle.

terms.name: Heel hook
terms.definition: Rotational leg lock attacking the knee through heel control.

terms.name: Kneebar
terms.definition: Straight leg lock attacking knee extension.

terms.name: Toe hold
terms.definition: Figure-four foot lock that rotates the ankle and can pressure the knee.

terms.name: Wristlock
terms.definition: Joint lock attacking wrist flexion, extension, or rotation.
